behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-model diarization/labeling output, which can vary by audio conditions and model behavior. A mocked transcript would hide real speaker-label failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model boundary (speech-to-text label preservation), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert per-utterance speaker labels are present and preserved in the handoff payload used by summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/mapping logic on transcript text and schema fields within a local code boundary (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor applies). No real OS/network/subprocess or model-quality dependence is required. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff to surface the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge phrasing) and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text in the summary input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: The pass condition depends on what the real LLM includes/omits in generated action items for assigned attendees (LLM-DEP). Stubbed outputs can always satisfy coverage and miss real omission failures. Rejected Workflow: this can be validated with one real summarization step from a prepared transcript; multiple agentic stages are not required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a real transcript with known attendee-task assignments to the production summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in live output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/orchestration: once decisions are identified, routing into the correct schema section must survive wiring between extraction output and formatter. Integration is sufficient because no real OS/network/subprocess is required, no real model judgment is required, and no multi-agentic chain is required (Integration-brake checks a/b/c/d all no). Rejected Agentic: behavior text says 'identified decisions', disqualifying dependence on model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision-identification output into the real schema-mapping layer in-process and assert all identified decisions land under `decisions` and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests whether the real summarization model correctly recognizes and places key decisions from transcript content (LLM-DEP). Deterministic fixtures or mocked model output would hide extraction/placement quality failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be exercised with a transcript-to-summary model call; it does not require multiple real agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcript cases containing explicit decision statements to the production LLM summarizer and assert those decisions appear in `decisions` section and are absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing with repeated model/API calls across segments plus merge coherence, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence (MULTI-STEP). Mocking segment results can hide ordering, carryover, and merge-quality failures across the chain. Rejected Agentic: behavior text requires split -> sequential process -> merge pipeline, not a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60 minute recording through production segmentation and summarization flow; assert segment order is preserved, all segments are processed, and merged output is coherent and complete as one structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline with multiple real agentic stages (transcription + LLM summarization) and cross-stage handoff, so Workflow is the minimum boundary to catch real failures. Lower levels cannot expose full-chain breakage or stage interaction failures. Rejected System: behavior text requires complete multi-stage pipeline, not just one infrastructure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file (upload through delivery endpoint) and assert successful completion with valid structured output containing required sections and no manual recovery steps.
